name: ttl-language
description: TTL (Tera Term Language) language support: syntax highlighting, completions, hover docs, and label navigation.

enumDescriptions: Detect language from VS Code UI setting
ttl.language.description: Language for hover documentation. 'auto' uses the VS Code display language.

ttl.maxNestingDepth.description: Maximum allowed block nesting depth before a warning is reported. Set to 0 to disable the check.

ttl.diagnostics.undefinedLabel.description: Warn when goto/call references a label with no matching definition (includes are resolved). Suppressed automatically when an included file cannot be resolved.

ttl.diagnostics.unknownCommand.description: Warn when a line starts with an unknown command, suggesting the closest known command.

ttl.diagnostics.duplicateLabel.description: Warn when the same label is defined more than once.

ttl.teraTermDir.markdownDescription: Directory that contains Tera Term's executables (`ttpmacro.exe` and `ttermpro.exe`), used to run the current `.ttl` file. When empty, common install locations such as `C:\Program Files\teraterm5` are tried automatically. Example: `C:\Program Files\teraterm5`.

enumDescriptions: Launch via ttermpro.exe /M= — starts Tera Term first, so terminal commands like clearscreen work before the macro calls connect.
Launch ttpmacro.exe directly — macro engine only; the terminal is linked when the macro calls connect.
ttl.runMacroVia.markdownDescription: How to run a macro. `teraterm` uses `ttermpro.exe /M=<file>` so terminal commands like `clearscreen` work without first calling `connect`. `ttpmacro` runs `ttpmacro.exe <file>` directly. Both executables are taken from `#ttl.teraTermDir#`.
